trust_assumption: The organization trusts the internet-facing VPN/Citrix/email/firewall appliance as a hardened security control whose pre-authentication request handling is itself a defended boundary.
violation: An unauthenticated request to the perimeter device's own parser reaches code execution, turning the security control into the first foothold inside the perimeter it was meant to protect.

techniques.name: Edge appliance pre-auth RCE (VPN/Citrix/Ivanti/Edge gateway CVEs)

trust_assumption: An internet-exposed RDP or SSH listener trusts that any party completing its authentication handshake is the credential's legitimate owner.
violation: An attacker reaching the exposed service guesses, sprays, or replays a credential and is granted an interactive remote session indistinguishable from the real operator's.

techniques.name: Exposed RDP/SSH brute-force and credential reuse

trust_assumption: Every authentication surface trusts that a valid username/password pair is presented only by the human it was issued to, independent of where or how it was obtained.
violation: Breach-corpus, sprayed, or stuffed credentials are wielded by the wrong party to log in as a legitimate user with no exploit and no anomaly at the auth layer.

techniques.name: Valid accounts via breach creds, password spray, credential stuffing

trust_assumption: The browser trusts content served by a site the user chose to visit as passive media safe to render, parse, and JIT.
violation: A compromised or attacker-controlled page (watering hole) feeds the rendering/scripting engine input that crosses into native code execution on the visitor's host during ordinary browsing.

trust_assumption: The host trusts a device enumerating as a USB keyboard to be a physically present human typing in real time.
violation: A scripted HID-emulating implant (Rubber Ducky, Bash Bunny, malicious cable) injects keystrokes at machine speed, executing operator-chosen commands under whatever session is unlocked at the console.

techniques.name: USB drop and HID keystroke injection (Rubber Ducky, Bash Bunny, O.MG cable)

trust_assumption: The physical access control treats proximity, a presented badge, or following an authorized person through a door as proof of an authorized identity.
violation: A cloned RFID badge, a picked lock, or a tailgated entry puts an unauthorized body inside the trusted physical perimeter where network drops and consoles are assumed to be operated by vetted staff.

trust_assumption: A client device trusts that an SSID and access point advertising a known network name is the legitimate infrastructure it previously associated with.
violation: A rogue AP / Wi-Fi Pineapple spoofs the trusted SSID, causing clients to auto-associate and route traffic through attacker-controlled infrastructure for capture, downgrade, or credential harvesting.

techniques.name: Rogue access point / evil twin (Wi-Fi Pineapple)

trust_assumption: The host trusts its signed, allowlisted command interpreters (PowerShell, cmd, WMIC, Bash, Python, Perl) as legitimate administrative tools regardless of the logic they are handed at runtime.
violation: An attacker feeds arbitrary in-memory script to a trusted interpreter, executing offensive logic under the interpreter's reputation without ever dropping a flagged binary (living off the land).

techniques.name: Command and scripting interpreter abuse (PowerShell, cmd, WMIC, Bash, Python, Perl)

trust_assumption: The OS trusts direct native/system-call invocations as the legitimate intent of a running process, since the API itself carries no notion of who supplied the instruction stream.
violation: Malicious code calls the native API directly to spawn threads and execute payloads beneath the userland hooks and command-line telemetry that detection assumes every execution must traverse.

trust_assumption: A managed host trusts patch content delivered over its update channel (WSUS) as authoritative and runs it with system privilege because it arrived from the configured update server.
violation: An attacker controlling the update channel or an unencrypted/forgeable update path injects a malicious package the client installs and executes as a privileged trusted update.

trust_assumption: A document is trusted as data to be read, even though the Office file format permits it to carry embedded VBA/XLM that the application will execute on open.
violation: The opened document's embedded macro is parsed as executable code, turning an attachment treated as content into host execution once the user enables it.

trust_assumption: The Service Control Manager trusts the binary path and configuration recorded for a service and runs it at boot with the service account's elevated context.
violation: A newly created or hijacked service (writable binary path, weak permissions) inherits SYSTEM-level execution every boot, persisting independently of the access used to install it.

techniques.name: New service creation and service binary/path hijack

trust_assumption: The WMI subsystem trusts a registered event filter-to-consumer binding as a legitimate management automation that fires its consumer action under SYSTEM whenever its event condition is met.
violation: A planted permanent WMI event subscription executes attacker logic on an arbitrary trigger (logon, time, process start) entirely inside the WMI repository, with no file or autostart entry to inspect.

trust_assumption: The loader trusts the OS DLL search order to resolve a dependency name to the legitimate library, and a signed host process trusts whatever module answers that name.
violation: An attacker plants a malicious DLL earlier in the search path (or beside a signed binary) so a trusted, signed process loads and executes attacker code under its own reputation (sideloading).

techniques.name: DLL search-order hijacking and DLL sideloading

trust_assumption: The boot chain trusts pre-OS code (firmware, bootloader, MBR/VBR, early kernel) as the foundational root of trust that loads before any defense the OS would run.
violation: A bootkit or kernel rootkit installs beneath the OS so it executes first, subverting the integrity measurements and security products that assume they run on an uncompromised boot chain.

trust_assumption: An identity system trusts the standing authenticators attached to an account (authorized_keys entries, IAM access keys, role trust policies) as configuration set only by legitimately authorized principals.
violation: An attacker who reached a privileged context adds its own SSH key or cloud IAM credential, minting a durable independent authenticator that survives password resets and the original intrusion.

techniques.name: Account manipulation: SSH authorized_keys and cloud IAM credential addition

trust_assumption: A web server trusts files within its document root as content it should serve and, for handler extensions, execute on request as the application's own code.
violation: A planted script in the web root is parsed and run by the server's interpreter, giving any attacker who knows the URL a persistent command channel running as the web service account.

trust_assumption: Kerberos services trust any TGT or service ticket whose PAC is correctly signed with the krbtgt account key (TGT) or the target service account key (TGS), validating the signature rather than re-checking the principal.
violation: An attacker holding the krbtgt or service key forges a Golden or Silver Ticket asserting arbitrary identity and group membership, and every service accepts the forged provenance as a domain-issued ticket.

techniques.name: Golden Ticket and Silver Ticket forgery
